behavior_in_task_description.reason: All required file paths, function names (artifactUrl, exists, get, getStream, put, healthCheck, buildImage), flag names (--force), URL path formats (/api/v8/artifacts/, /v8/artifacts/), query params (teamId, slug), env vars (TURBO_API, TURBO_TEAM), and config file changes are named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes the architecture and API contract but does not give line-level edits; agent must still design the implementation.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests verify behavior (URL format, exports, flags, script execution) rather than specific variable names or internal helpers; alternative correct implementations would pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests invoke node subprocesses to execute the modules (test_artifact_url_vercel, test_turbo_cache_exports, test_docker_image_cache_cli_flags) and parse JSON config files.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests assert specific URL substrings like '/api/v8/artifacts/deadbeef1234' and 'teamId=team_abc123' which require real implementation logic.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Tests cover URL formats for both vercel/self-hosted, team parameter, required exports, removal of turbo task, shebangs, built-in-only imports, and --force flag parsing.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass) and labeled with [static]/[pr_diff]/[repo_tests] tags.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code/config-focused, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names (turbo-cache.mjs, docker-image-cache.js, TURBO_API, artifactUrl) are consistent between instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction references files by role (which is necessary since the task is to rewrite them) without leaking exact line-level fixes.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Gold diff to package.json and turbo.jsonc is a localized removal of the build-docker-image entry, not a full-file rewrite.
